# Background

Artificial intelligence is advancing at an unprecedented pace. While our company has provided GitHub Copilot as a coding assistant, we lack other use cases due to the absence of LLM APIs that adhere to our stringent data protection regulations. Until recently, the company provided the <mention-doc token="SGGLw5MoSi32eBk8lgElgf97gXc" type="wiki">Model Garden User Guide</mention-doc> and <mention-doc token="GXMeweW3Hic2Bxk5lEXlBhA6gqd" type="wiki">n8n User Guide</mention-doc>. Now using the LLM API to enhance and automate our workflow has become possible.

## Objective

To discuss how we can integrate LLM API into our existing workflow to enhance our work productivity. What are the use cases, how to design and implement them.

## Possible Use cases

### AI GitLab MR review

We can use AI to code review automatically. The code review result can be added to the MR as comment. When the approver reviews the MR, he can check the AI review as a reference. This can enhance our MR review efficiency and quality.

#### Implementation

I already implemented a PoC on our MAB and MGR ocean repository. The implementation reuses our [DE Lark App backend](https%3A%2F%2Fsource.golabs.io%2Fgo-fin-ds%2Flending-data-lark-app) as backend service for providing a webhook API for GitLab CI task to trigger the MR review flow and invoking the LLM API. To enable the AI MR review flow, repository maintainers need to update the gitlab CI config([guide](https%3A%2F%2Fsource.golabs.io%2Fgofin-de%2Fci-common-utils)) to trigger a CI task when a new MR is created. The MR review flow is as follows.

#### AI MR Review Flow chart

<whiteboard token="D8MqwyUVFhKdI4bYeBblzR1fgHf"/>

Code review example:

https://source.golabs.io/ocean/p-mgrdata-gl/-/merge_requests/1787

<image token="LK1JbSU6joDg4qxqyaPlap9lgae" width="1273" height="3215" align="center"/>

Another example for MAB optimus repo:

https://source.golabs.io/ocean/p-mabdata-gl/-/merge_requests/2476

- Why reuse DE lark App backend as backend service
  - This service can provide public accessible Webhook API needed by GitLab. The webhook API is protected by access token.
  - We can use the existing features in this service to add Lark interaction.  Users can use `/review_mr https://path/to/mr` to trigger MR review manually.
    <image token="Ihnhbln82oREWFxV8VUlIHO7gOg" width="1353" height="929" align="center"/>

Current issues:

- This LLM API is not stable, and it sometimes takes a long time for response.
- The MR review is lengthy and lacks sufficient specific information about our business and technical specifications; we need to continuously tune the prompt. But at least now it can help to find out some issues like typos.

### User lark question classification automation

Every day, our users will raise questions in our lending Lending Data Lark group and mention our LendingData bot to get PIC to handle the question . Although users can add tags like `#ae, #mlops ` when mention  LendingData bot to get a more specific PIC. But no user is using this feature.  So we can add LLM base question classification automation. We can ask LLM to categorize the user question, when we use the category to get the specific PIC. Possible category examples:

<lark-table rows="3" cols="3" column-widths="292,100,100">

  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      Question example
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      Category
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      PICs
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      Hi, I have an issue with access xx MC table, although I applied access in the data console.
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      data access
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      <mention-user id="ou_32eea683d66576ee9c831ec0e5dc4374"/>and DWH oncall
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      Hi, can you help to ingest table xx from MySQL to mc
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      Data ingestion
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      xxx
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
</lark-table>

<image token="ZOTvbZZyForFGHxJ5KClJmAHgmD" width="2348" height="1172" align="center"/>

### Pagerduty Incident auto triage

Pagerdy incident triage can be a burden for us, especially for Optimus job alerts that happen at midnight or early morning. We already integrate pagerduty incident with lark via lark app backend.  

The first thing we can do is to build some tools that can be invoked by the lark message to help us triage the incident, even though we don't have a computer on hand. Some possible commands for example:

<lark-table rows="4" cols="2" column-widths="250,189">

  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      command
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      description
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      /optimus job_status
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      Get the optimis job information for the incident information. Return the latest optimus job status, retry times, log url
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      /optimus job_log
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      Get the latest airflow log
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
  <lark-tr>
    <lark-td>
      /mc desc
    </lark-td>
    <lark-td>
      Use delib to interact with MC. Return to the optimum job mc table information.
    </lark-td>
  </lark-tr>
</lark-table>

Maybe turn our lark backend service as a Model Context Protocal(MCP) service including the above tool.

Then we can build our LLM agent flow (maybe on n8n), possible flow:

<whiteboard token="WGX4wM2wDhdXTlbs7X9lPF0rgiG"/>

### User query optimization suggestion

Sometimes our user queries are not optimized, causing queries to be slow and using a lot of MaxCompute resources. We can provide a lark bot command for use to optimize their query.

Users can send their query to Lark Bot, the lark bot backend will use LLM to provide optimization suggestions to users.

### MaxCompute SQL generator

<mention-user id="ou_35800135da6d7d2c973387f08d690a3c"/>already developed this project: https://source.golabs.io/gofin-de/bi/maxcompute-sql-generator . It has integrated with third-party LLM providers, maxcompute. It has mcp, cli and web ui features. We can think about how we can combine this project with our Lark App backend. I think using Lark for interaction is more convenient for our users.

### MaxCompute Query Optimizer

To optimize MaxCompute jobs with long execution times, we will retrieve daily job information from the information schema. For jobs exceeding a certain duration, an LLM will analyze their SQL queries to identify potential optimizations. If optimizations are found, a report will be generated for DE to review.  DE will then share this report with the job owner. We can optimize Quick BI jobs first, then Tableau and Optimus jobs.
